codeReview.summary: The implementation is solid and fully covers the feature requirements. The ShareOnSocial component correctly captures the bingo board as a PNG image using html2canvas, handles desktop (download + clipboard) and mobile (Web Share API) sharing paths, shows loading/error states, and is fully accessible with proper ARIA attributes. Board.tsx was cleanly updated with forwardRef for DOM access. Integration into game.tsx is clean with flex-wrap for responsive layout. 17 comprehensive unit tests cover all expected behaviors and edge cases. All 218 tests pass, typecheck and lint are clean.

issues.description: abortControllerRef is assigned (line 108) but never used — no abort logic, no cleanup, no signal passed to any API. This is dead code that could confuse future maintainers. Consider removing it or implementing actual abort functionality.

No useEffect cleanup for timerRef. If the component unmounts while a feedback/error timer is pending, setShareState and setErrorMsg will be called on an unmounted component. While React 18+ no longer warns about this, it's best practice to clean up timers in a useEffect return. Add a useEffect(() => () => { if (timerRef.current) clearTimeout(timerRef.current) }, []).

CSS class overlap: the base className always includes 'cursor-pointer', and the loading conditional adds 'cursor-not-allowed'. While CSS cascade means cursor-not-allowed wins (same specificity, later in string), it would be cleaner to conditionally apply cursor-pointer only when not loading.

Error message exposes internal html2canvas error details: `Failed to generate image: ${message}` where message is the raw error from html2canvas. For a client-side bingo game this is low risk, but a generic message would be safer in production code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: html2canvas was added as a new project dependency (package.json) for client-side DOM-to-image capture. AGENTS.md doesn't mention this library or the pattern of using refs for DOM capture, which future features might need.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json now includes 'html2canvas: ^1.4.1'. The ShareOnSocial component imports it directly. Board.tsx was converted to forwardRef to expose the DOM node.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The forwardRef pattern was applied to Board.tsx to allow parent components to access the DOM node. This is a useful pattern that isn't documented in AGENTS.md but could be needed for other components that require DOM access (e.g., print, export).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/Board.tsx was changed from a simple function component to forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, BoardProps> to support the boardRef prop in game.tsx.

summary: PASS. The social-image-sharing feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage (17 tests) and clean integration. All feature requirements are met: bingo-conditional visibility, html2canvas board capture, desktop download+clipboard, mobile Web Share API, loading/error states, keyboard accessibility, ARIA attributes, and coexistence with the existing Share button. Four non-blocking issues were identified: dead abortControllerRef code, missing timer cleanup on unmount, minor CSS class overlap, and raw error message exposure. None of these affect correctness or user-facing behavior.
